statement.headerDoc:
STEP 980 — Brocard's problem, extension to n ∈ [13, 20]
  =======================================================

  Companion to `CollatzRei/BrocardProblem.lean` (n ≤ 12, native_decide brute force).

  ★ Brocard 1876 / Ramanujan 1913 — OPEN 150 years:
    The only (n, m) with n! + 1 = m² are:
      n=4 → m=5   (25 = 5²)
      n=5 → m=11  (121 = 11²)
      n=7 → m=71  (5041 = 71²)
    No other solution is known; Berndt–Galway 2000 verified up to N = 10⁹.

  ★ Approach for n ≥ 13 ★
    Brute force `∀ m ≤ bound, m * m ≠ n! + 1` becomes infeasible for native_decide
    once bound exceeds ~10⁷ (n
